mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations, as indicated by a 2.5% year-over-year increase in CPIAUCSL, prompt central banks to tighten monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or Rank IC > 0.05 in inflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals robust supply-side expansion, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between INDPRO and GDPC1 shows Rank IC < 0.03, or 20d returns are negative when both series rise > 1.5% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market tightening, evidenced by UNRATE falling below 4%, increases wage pressures and corporate costs, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE < 4% during late-cycle regimes, or t-test p > 0.05 for negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity, driving equity valuations higher and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances when INDPRO rises > 5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.02 in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in residential construction (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between HOUST and RSAFS shows Rank IC < 0.03, or 60d returns are negative when both series rise > 3% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: A 200k+ month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a tightening labor market, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances when PAYEMS rises > 200k MoM, or Rank IC < 0.02 in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) triggers aggressive monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as higher discount rates weigh on equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL > 3%, or Rank IC > 0.04 in high-inflation periods.
